mechanism: The Kalecki-Minsky classification determines whether credit impulse deceleration is a vol transition signal. In Type A (fiscal-supported) environments, credit deceleration is benign because profits are sustained independently. In Type B (credit-supported) environments, credit deceleration is a warning signal. Conditioning on regime type improves signal hit rate from 3/5 to 3/3.
regime_dependence: Framework is regime-defining by construction; accuracy depends on correct regime classification which has a 2-3 month data lag
